paper.title: A patient with newly diagnosed breast cancer found to have mosaic TP53 likely pathogenic variant

author_details.name: Lauren J. Massingham
affiliations: Department of Pediatrics, Harvard Medical School

entities.name: Department of Pediatrics, Harvard Medical School
A patient with newly diagnosed breast cancer found to have mosaic TP53 likely pathogenic variant

evidence_entities.text: A 36‐year‐old, nulligravid woman with a history of controlled eosinophilic esophagitis, asthma, and dense fibrocystic breasts was referred to the Breast Health Center after abnormal screening mammography.
She completed a comprehensive 76‐gene germline genetic test, which revealed tumor protein p53 (TP53) likely pathogenic variant (LPV; c.716A>G; p.N239S).
The same specific TP53 LPV (c.716A>G [p.N239S]) was detected in all three tumors.
In our patient, the VAF for the TP53 c.716A>G in each lesion was as follows: left breast DCIS, 2.4%; right breast DCIS, 33.9%; and left phyllodes tumor, 60.8%.

notes: This case report describes a complex genetic diagnosis involving mosaic TP53 variant leading to Li-Fraumeni syndrome diagnosis
The variant allele frequency data provided strong evidence for mosaicism rather than germline mutation
Multiple diagnostic procedures were required to confirm the mosaic nature of the TP53 variant
